symptom: tingling and weakness in the hand worsened by raising the arm
hook: an extra rib was sprouting from the lowest neck vertebra where none should exist
whatYouSee: An extra rib grows from the seventh cervical vertebra just above the normal first rib.
whyItMatters: A cervical rib can compress the brachial plexus or subclavian vessels and cause thoracic outlet syndrome with neurological or vascular symptoms in the arm.
treatment: Mild cases are managed with physiotherapy; significant nerve or vascular compression is treated by surgical resection of the rib.
takeaway: An extra rib off the C7 vertebra is a cervical rib. It is a classic cause of thoracic outlet syndrome.

condition.keyFindings: a supernumerary rib arising from the C7 vertebra, ranging from a small bony stub to a complete extra rib above the first thoracic rib

generated.threadsAnswer:
Answer: Cervical rib

👀 What you see:
An extra rib grows from the seventh cervical vertebra just above the normal first rib.

🦴 Why it matters:
A cervical rib can compress the brachial plexus or subclavian vessels and cause thoracic outlet syndrome with neurological or vascular symptoms in the arm.

💊 Treatment:
Mild cases are managed with physiotherapy; significant nerve or vascular compression is treated by surgical resection of the rib.

📝 Takeaway:
An extra rib off the C7 vertebra is a cervical rib. It is a classic cause of thoracic outlet syndrome.
